title: teucrium polium subsp. carthaginense
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. carthaginense, also known as North African germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium polium plant, a type of perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subsp. carthaginense include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems.
